concerns.text: FR-003 specifies 'ordinary least squares regression of distance modulus against log(z)'. The idea explicitly requires addressing 'local bulk flows' and 'selection effects' which masquerade as anisotropy. Standard OLS on log(z) does not account for peculiar velocities (bulk flows) which dominate at low redshift (z < 0.15). The spec must either mandate a peculiar velocity correction model (e.g., using a flow model) or explicitly state that the analysis is limited to a redshift range where bulk flows are negligible, otherwise the method cannot validly answer the research question regarding intrinsic anisotropy.

FR-005 requires comparing observed dipole/quadrupole against a null distribution generated by 'randomizing supernova positions'. This methodological approach fails to account for the 'selection effects in the Pantheon sample' explicitly raised in the idea's objection. Randomizing positions ignores the actual survey mask and selection function (which is not isotropic). The null model must instead randomize within the *observed* selection function or use a realistic mock catalog that includes the survey's selection effects, otherwise the p-value will be invalid.
